brief_justification: Arrives at the exact correct answer (Yes) with valid multi-layered reasoning; traces norm violation, causal mechanism, counterfactual logic, and cites psychological research (Knobe & Fraser) showing 3+ levels of analysis; proceeds mostly linearly without explicitly naming biases or reflecting on its own reasoning method, though it does pivot to address Alex's role; applies an explicit counterfactual check ('if Benni had followed instructions') which qualifies as basic verification from a second angle; uses mild hedging ('likely') but does not formally distinguish assumptions from facts or state what would change the conclusion; briefly addresses Alex as an alternative causal candidate and explains why he would not be held responsible, but does not enumerate or systematically reject other framings; every conclusion is traceable to an explicit premise and a third party could reproduce the full chain, though the Knobe & Fraser citation is asserted without derivation.
